# terrible_f5networks_f5_modules_bigiq_utility_license_assignment (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `device` (String) When `managed` is `false`, specifies the address, or hostname, where the BIG-IQ can reach the remote device to register. When `managed` is `true`, specifies the managed device, or device UUID, you want to register. If `managed` is `true`, it is very important you do not have more than one device with the same name. BIG-IQ internally recognizes devices by their ID, and therefore, this module cannot guarantee the correct device will be registered. The device returned is the device that is used.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `key` (String) The registration key from which you want choose an offering.
- `offering` (String) Name of the license offering to assign to the device.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `device_password` (String) The password of the `device_username`. When `managed` is `false`, this parameter is required.
- `device_port` (Number) Specifies the port of the remote device to connect to. If this parameter is not specified, the default is `443`.
- `device_username` (String) The username used to connect to the remote device. This username should be one that has sufficient privileges on the remote device to do licensing. Usually this is the `Administrator` role. When `managed` is `false`, this parameter is required.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `managed` (Boolean) Whether the specified device is a managed or un-managed device. When `state` is `present`, this parameter is required.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) When `present`, ensures the device is assigned the specified license. When `absent`, ensures the license is revokes from the remote device and freed on the BIG-IQ.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `unit_of_measure` (String) Sets the rate at which this license usage is billed. Depending on your license, you may have different units of measure available to you. If a particular unit is not available to you, the module notifies you at licensing time.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
